_provenance: v3 rewrite 2026-05-16: motion-packet pivot per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §1 (Murrow subsection) + §41 SPRINT_LOG checklist item 7. Briefing collapsed from eight-line case-summary (which named the motion to set aside and the winning frame in one breath) to four file-fact observations plus two doctrinal pointers (Tenancy Act notice-window citation, third-clause authority requirement). The motion-to-set-aside line moves to murrow_post_packet_motion_vehicle, gated on all four element_* bools. Two new decoy-decision states (murrow_first_meeting_decoy_notice_period, murrow_archive_walkthrough_decoy_merits) surface D1/D2 as named auxiliary theories the player may bolt on. A cold walk-back (murrow_post_decoy_incapacity) lands when decoy_incapacity is selected. Mrs. Borowski normalised to Mrs. Sikorska in court_readiness_check. Address forms per AGENTS.md §Address forms and Piotr's 2026-05-19 clarification: Murrow says 'Dr. A. Cula' exactly ONCE at first meeting (either before_pig in path A, or state_2_response_* in path B), then 'Cula' in every subsequent line — including formal contexts like court and client-facing scenes. 'Doctor Cula' is non-canonical and not used. Cula opens with 'Mr. Murrow' and only earns bare 'Murrow' after the friend-invitation calibration line, which is preserved verbatim in murrow_first_meeting.
_anti_ai_voice_pass: No contrastive antithesis; em-dashes capped per Murrow's archival register; no scrub-list vocabulary. 'Unkind' in murrow_post_decoy_incapacity is the only adjective in Murrow's chapter-1 catalogue and is load-bearing for the cold-pushback beat.

lines: Dr. A. Cula. Mr. Pig is expecting you. The case can wait the ten minutes Mr. Pig requires for opening remarks.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: [Mr. Murrow. Reading glasses, ink on the cuff, blue spines behind him in a column. Identify, hand off, do not bow.]

choices.text: Mr. Murrow. Dr. A. Cula. I was told you'd have a case for me.
Mr. Murrow. Dr. A. Cula. A pleasure to meet you.

lines: Well, well, well. Dr. A. Cula, the famous, promising and talented new hire.
Dr. A. Cula.
Murrow. Mr. Murrow.

states._comment: v3 motion-packet rewrite. Murrow gives the file as observation: four facts about what is in the docket, two doctrinal pointers, and the friend-invitation calibration. He does NOT name the procedural vehicle (motion to set aside) — that line moves to murrow_post_packet_motion_vehicle, gated on all four element_* bools. The Tenancy-Act citation surfaces as a pointer the player may later choose to deploy as D2; the third-clause authority requirement is the on-record E4 surfacing (he answers Cula's authority question with the doctrine, no editorial). on_dismiss sets met_murrow, the surfaced/binder flags Murrow has read aloud, and the two element bools his briefing establishes (E3 timely-notice and E4 no-third-party-cure). The element_non_current_address and element_landlord_knowledge bools stay false here and are written by Crab's post-binder state when Cula brings the binder back. Friend-invitation calibration verbatim. Asia-hedge verbatim.
lines: Welcome to Pig & Swine. You've picked a noisy day to begin.
The Sikorska eviction. Halina Sikorska, at number twelve. The notice in the file was served to number seven.
The building was renumbered in two thousand and fifteen. The two thousand and nineteen renewal in the file carries the landlord's countersignature.
Hearing ran ex parte. The current resident at number seven brought the notice down three weeks late. Actual notice the twenty-eighth of April.
Two doctrinal points worth your attention. The Tenancy Act sets a fourteen-day window from notice; check the math against the envelope dates yourself.
And the third clause of one-thirty-five-bis, on cure by a third party in occupation. The shelf has it in green pen.
Procedural binder, second shelf, blue spine. Rights memo clipped inside the front cover. Friday at fourteen hundred, courtroom four.
She has not formally retained yet. She is coming in this afternoon.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: The resident at number seven. Does his accepting the notice cure service?

lines: No. Express authority is required. He had none.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Thank you, Mr. Murrow.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Wrong-door service, fourteen days from actual notice, motion to set aside. I can carry that to Crab.

lines: It is Murrow, to friends. The 'Mister' I keep for invoices.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Then it's Cula.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: I am still deciding whether reception staff qualify as friends or as workplace constellations.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: She'll be relieved either way.

lines.speaker: murrow
lines.text: Binder first. Crab second. The shape is yours to name. Find me before fourteen hundred if anything in the binder reads wrong.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out (F4/F5):** Beat 4 dwell-prompt picker for Murrow. Scaffolded from cula.json::cula_b4_murrow_dwell_options. Post-friend-invitation address form: bare 'Murrow'. State-level speaker:'cula'; options chain to reply states that gate on chapter1.state_choice and clear it on dismiss. Ordered AFTER murrow_first_meeting and BEFORE post_briefing_pre_binder so the picker fires when state_choice is empty; repeats fall through when state_choice is non-empty or after dwell completes.
states.speaker: cula
lines: [A pause before going for the binder. Ask Murrow one more thing.]

choices.text: Murrow. Out of curiosity. How long have you been at Pig & Swine?
Murrow. The wrong-address mechanism. Have you seen this shape of defect before?
And before I meet her. What should I expect from Mrs. Sikorska?

Cula's prompt + TODO for Murrow's reply (Piotr to author per 'you author, I wire' decision 2026-05-25).

lines.text: Murrow. Out of curiosity. How long have you been at Pig & Swine?

Surfaces tenure without naming the Ch4 ledger content. The Mrożek paperback on the windowsill is in scope as a passing reference if it lands; the ledger is not.]

The renumbering is unusual; the wrong-address tactic is not.' Cula's prompt + TODO for Murrow's reply (Piotr to author).

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Murrow. The wrong-address mechanism. Have you seen this shape of defect before?

lines: [TODO 2026-05-25: Piotr to author Murrow's reply. Voice: short, declarative, archival. Two sentences in the canonical observation-then-implication rhythm. Surface that the wrong-address tactic is not novel; the 2015 renumbering is the wrinkle. Do not editorialize on landlord conduct.]

Cula's prompt + TODO for Murrow's reply (Piotr to author). The reply colours Cula's read going into Beat 8 — Murrow's read calibrates what stance the player will pick.

lines.text: And before I meet her. What should I expect from Mrs. Sikorska?

lines: [TODO 2026-05-25: Piotr to author Murrow's reply. Voice: archival-amused, one calibration detail. The line should give Cula (and the player) a small read on Halina without prescribing the stance choice. Aim at one biographical fact Murrow has noticed from the file rather than at a personality judgment.]
You're back without the binder. The shelf has not moved.

states._comment: v3 rewrite. HEAD line was 'Now Crab. Nothing else moves until service is checked.' — the second sentence pre-names the winning frame. v3 drops the conclusion and points Cula at Crab as the second pair of eyes; the shape is for Cula to name.
lines: Now Crab. Take the binder; let him read it with you. The shape is in the file. Crab is the second pair of eyes you want.

states.trigger: chapter1.met_murrow == true && chapter1.has_law_binder == true && chapter1.surfaced_tenancy_act_window == true && !chapter1.decoy_notice_period && !chapter1.court_ready && !chapter1.entered_court
states._comment: NEW state — D2 (notice-period failure) decision moment per PROPOSAL §1.2. Fires once after Cula has met Murrow and picked up the binder. Murrow names the Tenancy Act fourteen-day window again as a colorable auxiliary theory; the offer is procedurally credible, not endorsed. The options block writes chapter1.decoy_notice_period (true = include, false = leave out). once:true so the offer doesn't re-fire after the player commits. No chain — Murrow stays neutral on the choice and the player carries on.
lines: While you have the file open. The Tenancy Act window. Counsel sometimes plead it in the alternative when the dates run close.
It is procedurally subordinate to the locational defect. The bench may hear it; the bench may not. Your call whether to include it in the motion.

choices.text: Leave it out. The address defect ends the matter on its own.
Include it in the alternative. Belt and braces.

states._comment: NEW state — D1 (merits / substantive defense) decision moment per PROPOSAL §1.2. Fires once after archive research is complete. Murrow notes that Halina's payment receipts are real and asks whether to plead the merits as a backup. The offer is wrong-priority for the motion stage; Murrow's framing makes that visible without rejecting the option. once:true.
lines: One more question before we go. Mrs. Sikorska's receipts are in the file Crab brought back. The substantive case is real.
It is not before the court at the motion. It can be pleaded in the alternative if you want it on the record.

choices.text: Leave it. The merits live behind the procedural door.
Plead it as a backup. If the procedural angle slips, we want the merits in front of the judge.

states.trigger: chapter1.element_non_current_address == true && chapter1.element_landlord_knowledge == true && chapter1.element_timely_actual_notice_motion == true && chapter1.element_no_third_party_cure == true && chapter1.recruited_crab == true && !chapter1.decoy_incapacity && !chapter1.court_ready && !chapter1.entered_court
states._comment: NEW state — post-packet attachment of the procedural vehicle per PROPOSAL §1.4 (Murrow). Fires only when all four required elements have been surfaced (Crab's binder read-aloud sets E1/E2 and confirms E3; Murrow's briefing sets E3/E4; Crab's pre-binder stairwell observation also sets E4). Murrow now names what he refused to name in the briefing — the motion to set aside under Article 135-bis § 2, with the fourteen-day window. The line is reward, not preview. once:true. Trigger excludes decoy_incapacity so Murrow does not attach the motion warmly on a path he has cold-walked-back.
lines: Crab tells me the four parts are in. Address out of date; landlord on notice of the change; motion within fourteen days of actual notice; no third party who could cure.
We file the motion to set aside under Article one-thirty-five-bis, paragraph two. Fourteen days from actual notice. We are inside the window.
Hearing rescheduled, merits preserved. That is the shape. Mrs. Sikorska is at one. You will lead.

states._comment: NEW state — cold walk-back when decoy_incapacity is set. Murrow's voice here is colder than in any other state. 'Unkind' is the only adjective in his chapter-1 catalogue and is load-bearing for the in-fiction pushback per the task brief. The second clause ('procedurally wrong') keeps the rebuke doctrinal rather than personal — the firm's moral compass is rule-shaped, not sentimental. Murrow strips Cula's lead role and re-files the motion himself; the chapter still ships, the cost is loud. Address form 'Cula' per Piotr 2026-05-19 — Murrow uses 'Cula' in every state after the first meeting, regardless of register; the cold tone is carried by content, not by reverting the form. once:true.
lines: Crab tells me you proposed incapacity.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: She is elderly. The service —

lines: She is elderly. She is not incompetent. The argument is unkind and procedurally wrong. I will file the motion on the service defect myself; you will not argue incapacity in my courtroom.
Mrs. Sikorska is at one. When she arrives, you will not raise her age. Is that clear, Cula.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Clear.

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — S-grade. Voice draft from minigames.txt §Character reactions §Murrow Perfect ('Acceptable.'). Spec note: 'This should be treated as immense praise.' Murrow's one-word approval is the entire joke; no address-form needed on a single word.
lines: Acceptable.

states._comment: V1.4 coffee reaction — D/F-grade. Voice verbatim from minigames.txt §Character reactions §Murrow Bad ('You have created a beverage with procedural defects.'). Opens with 'Cula' per Piotr 2026-05-19: Murrow uses 'Dr. A. Cula' only at first meeting, 'Cula' in every subsequent state.
lines: Cula. You have created a beverage with procedural defects.

states.trigger: chapter1.has_law_binder == true && chapter1.has_rights_memo == true && chapter1.recruited_crab == true && chapter1.recruited_whimsy == true && !chapter1.court_ready
states._comment: v3 minor edit. Mrs. Borowski → Mrs. Sikorska (provisional title flagged in the prior version's _comment_borowski_title resolved here; the case is Sikorska throughout). The 'order set' line stays Murrow's; v3 leaves the procedural order in Murrow's mouth because by this point Cula has named the shape (or not) and Murrow's confirmation is the architectural beat. **2026-05-25 critique F1:** verbless fragment list (back-to-back 'On file.' / 'Confirmed.' / 'Courtroom four.') replaced with one archival-amused sentence pairing binder and memo. Pig's maritime aside trimmed per critique F11 (kraken/bait cut; printer-lease detail preserved in plain register).

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Murrow. We have service, fairness, and a modest remedy. Please tell me that is a case and not just a hopeful table of contents.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Walk route, court time, who carries what. Run me through the checklist.

lines: Binder and memo, both on file. Two months ago neither could be found; this is, by the standards of this office, an organisational triumph.
Crab and Whimsy are on the appearance. Fourteen hundred, courtroom four.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: The printer lease has come back through the postal service rather than the leasing company, which I am told is also a development.

lines.speaker: pig
lines.text: When I was an apprentice, we had no readiness check. Only fear. We navigated by panic alone.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Understood, Mr. Pig. We will keep the printer informed.

lines: Order: service first, fair hearing second, remedy last. We leave at thirteen-thirty.

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Asia, has Mrs. Sikorska been told we're coming?

lines.speaker: asia
lines.text: She rang an hour ago, Dr. A. Cula. She'll be there at quarter to.

states._comment: **2026-05-25 fan-out (F4/F5):** Beat 13 post-victory exchange with Murrow, paired with cula.json::cula_b13_brief_murrow_response. Per chapter_1.md §322: dry-collegial; checklist-style remark about the next case. Cula's reply ('Murrow. Send me what you have on the next file. Tomorrow morning is fine.') is inlined; Murrow's question line is the TODO Piotr authors. The b13_complete flag write is the responsibility of whichever Beat 13 state runs last in the sequence; this state does not write it.
lines: [TODO 2026-05-25: Piotr to author Murrow's brief post-victory line. Voice: dry-collegial. One short forward-looking sentence — what is on Murrow's desk after Sikorska clears. The Ch4 ledger plant lives in cula.json::cula_b13_murrow_ledger_silent and remains silent; do not write it here.]

lines.speaker: cula
lines.text: Murrow. Send me what you have on the next file. Tomorrow morning is fine.

idle_flavor: The paperwork is current. Whether it is correct is a separate question.
Ensure every form is stamped in triplicate.
Two pages are out of sequence. Not yours.
